attributes.action: Lower Vehicle Excise Duty and raise the £40,000 ‘luxury car tax’ threshold
attributes.additional_details: The £40,000 threshold has not changed since 2017. Adjusted for inflation it should exceed £54,000. Ordinary family cars now routinely breach this limit, triggering a £410 annual surcharge for five years. This is not a luxury tax — it is an outdated stealth tax hitting everyday drivers who already face heavy burdens through fuel duty, insurance premium tax, and standard VED.
attributes.background: Raise the £40,000 expensive car supplement threshold to at least £50,000 to reflect inflation, and conduct a full review of VED rates to ensure they are fair and do not penalise ordinary motorists buying mainstream family vehicles.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petitions which call for similar actions:

Increase the Luxury Car Tax threshold to £50-55,000 
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/751452

Require VED Expensive Car Supplement threshold to be uprated with inflation https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/767460
